Task / Use Case: Architecture design for schedule viewer refactor — CSV import, hierarchical team/VS filtering, per-day navigation, color coding, current-time indicator
AI Usage Description: Explored full codebase (index.html, script.js, styles.css, rooms.json, room2.json). Identified 4 key limitations (single-team filter, no multi-day, JSON-only, limited CSS). Evaluated 3 design approaches (ES Modules multi-file, single-file expansion, global namespace multi-script). Produced architecture design document with canonical event schema, 6 module specifications (loader, filter, url-state, renderer, time-indicator, app orchestrator), data flow diagram, hierarchical filter logic, color palette, error handling strategy, testing strategy, and risk analysis. Saved to artifacts/2026-03-17-schedule-architecture.md with handoff for @ema-planner.
Outcome: Working code produced — architecture document complete with all sections; requirements artifact and architecture artifact saved to artifacts/
Estimated Impact: Time saved ≈ 50-60% — AI handled codebase exploration, data model analysis, approach comparison, and document generation rapidly; human review still needed for correctness of filter logic interpretation, VS naming conventions, and architectural trade-off validation (~45min AI-assisted vs ~1.5h fully manual)
